2083	// TestExecuteBeadInvalidBeadID verifies that beadIDs with characters illegal
2084	// in git ref names are rejected with a clear error before any git or agent
2085	// operations are attempted.
2086	func TestExecuteBeadInvalidBeadID(t *testing.T) {
2087		invalidIDs := []string{
2088			"bead with spaces",
2089			"bead~1",
2090			"bead^1",
2091			"bead:name",
2092			"bead[0]",
2093		}
2094		for _, id := range invalidIDs {
2095			t.Run(id, func(t *testing.T) {
2096				git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111"}
2097				runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
2098				f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
2099	
2100				root := f.NewRootCommand()
2101				_, cmdErr := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", id)
2102				require.Error(t, cmdErr)
2103				assert.Contains(t, cmdErr.Error(), "invalid bead ID")
2104	
2105				// No git or agent operations should have been attempted.
2106				assert.Empty(t, git.addedWTs, "no worktree should be created for invalid bead ID")
2107			})
2108		}
2109	}
2110	
2111	// TestExecuteBeadEvidenceFields verifies that runtime evidence fields are
2112	// populated in the JSON output.
2113	func TestExecuteBeadEvidenceFields(t *testing.T) {
2114		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
2115			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
2116			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222",
2117		}
2118		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{
2119			ExitCode: 0,
2120			Harness:  "testharness",
2121			Model:    "test-model",
2122			Tokens:   42,
2123			CostUSD:  0.001,
2124		}}
2125		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
2126	
2127		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
2128	
2129		assert.Equal(t, "testharness", res.Harness)
2130		assert.Equal(t, "test-model", res.Model)
2131		assert.Equal(t, 42, res.Tokens)
2132		assert.InDelta(t, 0.001, res.CostUSD, 1e-9)
2133		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.SessionID)
2134		assert.False(t, res.StartedAt.IsZero())
2135		assert.False(t, res.FinishedAt.IsZero())
2136		assert.Equal(t, "aaaa1111", res.BaseRev)
2137		assert.Equal(t, "bbbb2222", res.ResultRev)
2138	}
2139	
2140	// TestExecuteBeadModelFlagPassthrough locks in the resolution contract for
2141	// execute-bead's model option: the value supplied via ExecuteBeadOptions.Model
2142	// is passed verbatim to the runner, and an empty value is not silently replaced
2143	// by any hardcoded or catalog-derived default. This regression test guards
2144	// against routing layers injecting a model (e.g. a stale vendor/model like
2145	// "z-ai/glm-5.1") when the caller did not request one — the case the agent
2146	// harness resolves from ~/.config/agent/config.yaml must be preserved by
2147	// ExecuteBead handing the runner an empty Model so the harness's own
2148	// resolution chain runs.
2149	func TestExecuteBeadModelFlagPassthrough(t *testing.T) {
2150		t.Run("empty model stays empty through ExecuteBead", func(t *testing.T) {
2151			git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
2152				mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
2153				wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222",
2154			}
2155			runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
2156			f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
2157	
2158			// No --model flag supplied to execute-bead.
2159			runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
2160	
2161			assert.Equal(t, "", runner.last.Model,
2162				"runner must receive an empty Model when no --model flag is provided; "+
2163					"any non-empty value here indicates a routing layer injected a default, "+
2164					"which would override the harness's own config-driven resolution")
2165		})
2166	
2167		t.Run("explicit model is forwarded verbatim", func(t *testing.T) {
2168			git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
2169				mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
2170				wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222",
2171			}
2172			runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
2173			f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
2174	
2175			runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead", "--model", "qwen3.5-27b")
2176	
2177			assert.Equal(t, "qwen3.5-27b", runner.last.Model,
2178				"runner must receive the exact --model value the caller passed")
2179		})
2180	}
2181	
2182	func TestExecuteBeadStatusMapping(t *testing.T) {
2183		cases := []struct {
2184			name     string
2185			result   agent.ExecuteBeadResult
2186			expected string
2187		}{
2188			{
2189				name:     "merged success",
2190				result:   agent.ExecuteBeadResult{Outcome: "merged", ExitCode: 0},
2191				expected: agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
2192			},
2193			{
2194				name:     "no changes stays non-success",
2195				result:   agent.ExecuteBeadResult{Outcome: "no-changes", ExitCode: 0},
2196				expected: agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges,
2197			},
2198			{
2199				name:     "execution failure dominates preserved outcome",
2200				result:   agent.ExecuteBeadResult{Outcome: "preserved", ExitCode: 1, Reason: "agent execution failed"},
2201				expected: agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
2202			},

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"os/exec"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"strconv"
10		"strings"
11		"sync"
12		"testing"
13	
14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
15		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
16	)
17	
18	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
19	// Git helpers
20	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
21	
22	// scrubbedGitEnvInteg returns the current environment with all GIT_* variables
23	// removed, ensuring test-local git subprocesses don't inherit parent repo state.
24	func scrubbedGitEnvInteg() []string {
25		parent := os.Environ()
26		env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
27		for _, kv := range parent {
28			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
29				continue
30			}
31			env = append(env, kv)
32		}
33		return env
34	}
35	
36	// runGitInteg runs a git command in dir with scrubbed GIT_* env.
37	// Fails the test on non-zero exit.
38	func runGitInteg(t *testing.T, dir string, args ...string) string {
39		t.Helper()
40		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
41		cmd.Dir = dir
42		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
43		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
44		if err != nil {
45			t.Fatalf("git %v in %s: %v\n%s", args, dir, err, out)
46		}
47		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
48	}
49	
50	// runGitIntegOutput runs a git command and returns (output, error) — for cases
51	// where failure is expected or handled by the caller.
52	func runGitIntegOutput(dir string, args ...string) (string, error) {
53		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
54		cmd.Dir = dir
55		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
56		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
57		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err
58	}
59	
60	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
61	// Repo setup
62	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
63	
64	// newScriptHarnessRepo creates a temp git repo with an initial seed commit on
65	// main, seeds .ddx/beads.jsonl with beadCount open beads (IDs like
66	// ddx-int-0001, ddx-int-0002 …), and returns (projectRoot, initialSHA).
67	// The initialSHA is the SHA immediately after the "chore: initial seed" commit,
68	// BEFORE the seed-beads commit, so callers can measure commits added beyond it.
69	func newScriptHarnessRepo(t *testing.T, beadCount int) (string, string) {
70		t.Helper()
71	
72		root := t.TempDir()
73	
74		runGitInteg(t, root, "init", "-b", "main")
75		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
76		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
77	
78		// Create initial seed file and commit so the repo has a HEAD.
79		seedFile := filepath.Join(root, "seed.txt")
80		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(seedFile, []byte("seed\n"), 0644))
81		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".")
82		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
83	
84		initialSHA := runGitInteg(t, root, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
85	
86		// Set up .ddx dir and bead store.
87		ddxDir := filepath.Join(root, ".ddx")
88		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
89		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
90		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
91	
92		for i := 0; i < beadCount; i++ {
93			id := fmt.Sprintf("ddx-int-%04d", i+1)
94			b := &bead.Bead{
95				ID:        id,
96				Title:     fmt.Sprintf("Integration test bead %d", i+1),
97				IssueType: "task",
98				Priority:  i,
99			}
100			require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
101		}
102	
103		// Commit beads.jsonl so the initial worktree contains it.
104		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
105		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: seed beads")
106	
107		return root, initialSHA
108	}
109	
110	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
111	// Directive file helper
112	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
113	
114	// writeDirectiveFile writes a directive file at path with the given lines.
115	func writeDirectiveFile(t *testing.T, path string, lines []string) {
116		t.Helper()
117		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0755))
118		content := strings.Join(lines, "\n") + "\n"
119		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(path, []byte(content), 0644))
120	}
121	
122	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
123	// Executor helpers
124	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
125	
126	// landSerializerMu guards landSerializerMap.
127	var (
128		landSerializerMu  sync.Mutex
129		landSerializerMap = map[string]*sync.Mutex{}
130	)
131	
132	// landMutexFor returns the per-projectRoot mutex that serializes Land() calls.
133	// This mimics the production per-project LandCoordinator goroutine
134	// (cli/internal/server/workers.go). Tests that spawn concurrent workers share
135	// this mutex so the CAS-based UpdateRefTo in Land() never races.
136	func landMutexFor(projectRoot string) *sync.Mutex {
137		landSerializerMu.Lock()
138		defer landSerializerMu.Unlock()
139		m, ok := landSerializerMap[projectRoot]
140		if !ok {
141			m = &sync.Mutex{}
142			landSerializerMap[projectRoot] = m
143		}
144		return m
145	}
146	
147	// scriptHarnessExecutor builds an ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc that:
148	//  1. Runs ExecuteBead with the script harness (directive file = directivePath).
149	//  2. Passes the result through LandBeadResult with a LandingAdvancer that
150	//     calls Land() — serialized via landMutexFor(projectRoot).
151	//
152	// Both ExecuteBead and Land() are serialized per projectRoot. This is required
153	// because ExecuteBead calls CommitTracker (which acquires the git index lock)
154	// and because Land() uses CAS-based UpdateRefTo. In production, the server
155	// dispatches one bead per worker and serializes Land() via the LandCoordinator
156	// goroutine; in integration tests, the same mutex handles both.
157	//
158	// Failed exits (exit code != 0) with commits are preserved under
159	// refs/ddx/iterations/ by LandBeadResult before Land() is invoked.
160	func scriptHarnessExecutor(t *testing.T, projectRoot, directivePath string) ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc {
161		t.Helper()
162		// Build Runner once at executor-construction time so concurrent goroutine
163		// calls do not race on BuiltinCatalog (NewRunner is not goroutine-safe
164		// during catalog construction).
165		runner := NewRunner(Config{})
166		gitOps := &RealGitOps{}
167		orchGitOps := &RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
168		// Per-projectRoot mutex serializes git operations so concurrent workers
169		// don't race on the git index or on Land()'s CAS UpdateRefTo.
170		repoMu := landMutexFor(projectRoot)
171	
172		return ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
173			repoMu.Lock()
174			defer repoMu.Unlock()
175	
176			res, err := ExecuteBead(ctx, projectRoot, beadID, ExecuteBeadOptions{
177				Harness:     "script",
178				Model:       directivePath,
179				AgentRunner: runner,
180			}, gitOps)
181			if err != nil {
182				if res != nil {
183					return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
184				}
185				return ExecuteBeadReport{
186					BeadID: beadID,
187					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
188					Detail: err.Error(),
189				}, nil
190			}
191	
192			landing, landErr := LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, orchGitOps, BeadLandingOptions{
193				LandingAdvancer: func(r *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error) {
194					// landMutexFor is already held (repoMu == landMutexFor(projectRoot)).
195					req := BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, r)
196					return Land(projectRoot, req, RealLandingGitOps{})
197				},
198			})
199			if landErr != nil {
200				return ExecuteBeadReport{
201					BeadID: beadID,
202					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
203					Detail: landErr.Error(),
204				}, nil
205			}
206			ApplyLandingToResult(res, landing)
207			return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
208		})
209	}
210	
211	// executeBeadResultToReport converts ExecuteBeadResult to ExecuteBeadReport.
212	func executeBeadResultToReport(res *ExecuteBeadResult) ExecuteBeadReport {
213		return ExecuteBeadReport{
214			BeadID:             res.BeadID,
215			AttemptID:          res.AttemptID,
216			WorkerID:           res.WorkerID,
217			Harness:            res.Harness,
218			Model:              res.Model,
219			Status:             res.Status,
220			Detail:             res.Detail,
221			SessionID:          res.SessionID,
222			BaseRev:            res.BaseRev,
223			ResultRev:          res.ResultRev,
224			PreserveRef:        res.PreserveRef,
225			NoChangesRationale: res.NoChangesRationale,
226		}
227	}
228	
229	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
230	// Git assertion helpers
231	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
232	
233	// gitCommitCount returns the number of commits reachable from ref (plus any
234	// additional git rev-list args like "--not", "SHA").
235	func gitCommitCount(t *testing.T, projectRoot string, refAndArgs ...string) int {
236		t.Helper()
237		args := append([]string{"rev-list", "--count"}, refAndArgs...)
238		out := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, args...)
239		n, err := strconv.Atoi(out)
240		require.NoError(t, err, "git rev-list --count %v", refAndArgs)
241		return n
242	}
243	
244	// gitHasMergeCommits returns true when any merge commits exist in ref's history.
245	func gitHasMergeCommits(t *testing.T, projectRoot, ref string) bool {
246		t.Helper()
247		out := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "log", "--merges", "--format=%H", ref)
248		return out != ""
249	}
250	
251	// refExists returns true when the given git ref is present in the repo.
252	func refExists(t *testing.T, projectRoot, ref string) bool {
253		t.Helper()
254		_, err := runGitIntegOutput(projectRoot, "rev-parse", "--verify", ref)
255		return err == nil
256	}
257	
258	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
259	// Store helpers
260	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
261	
262	// makeLoopStore creates an ExecuteBeadLoopStore backed by a bead.Store rooted
263	// in ddxDir. The store is already initialised by newScriptHarnessRepo.
264	func makeLoopStore(t *testing.T, ddxDir string) ExecuteBeadLoopStore {
265		t.Helper()
266		return bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
267	}
268	
269	// countClosedBeads counts how many beads in ddxDir have status "closed".
270	func countClosedBeads(t *testing.T, ddxDir string) int {
271		t.Helper()
272		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
273		all, err := store.List("", "", nil)
274		require.NoError(t, err)
275		n := 0
276		for _, b := range all {
277			if b.Status == bead.StatusClosed {
278				n++
279			}
280		}
281		return n
282	}
283	
284	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
285	// Shell quoting helper
286	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
287	
288	// escapeForShell escapes a string for embedding in a single-quoted shell argument.
289	func escapeForShell(s string) string {
290		return strings.ReplaceAll(s, "'", `'\''`)
291	}
292

1654	// RealLandingGitOps via the script harness — no fakes for the components
1655	// under test. The script harness produces a real commit, then trips a
1656	// synthetic failure so the runner returns an error alongside the commit;
1657	// the orchestrator must preserve without attempting a merge.
1658	func TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorWithCommitsPreservesBeforeLand(t *testing.T) {
1659		workDir := t.TempDir()
1660	
1661		scrubEnv := func() []string {
1662			parent := os.Environ()
1663			env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
1664			for _, kv := range parent {
1665				if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
1666					continue
1667				}
1668				env = append(env, kv)
1669			}
1670			return env
1671		}
1672		runGit := func(args ...string) string {
1673			t.Helper()
1674			cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
1675			cmd.Dir = workDir
1676			cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
1677			out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
1678			require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
1679			return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
1680		}
1681	
1682		runGit("init", "-b", "main")
1683		runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
1684		runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
1685		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
1686		runGit("add", ".")
1687		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
1688	
1689		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
1690			ID:        "my-bead",
1691			Title:     "Test execute-bead agent error with commits preserves before land",
1692			Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
1693			IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
1694		})
1695		runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
1696		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead")
1697		baseSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
1698	
1699		// Script harness: create a real commit in the worker worktree, then
1700		// trip a synthetic failure so runScriptFn returns (result, execErr).
1701		// This mimics the original fakeAgentRunner{err: ..., result: nil} case
1702		// — runner errored after commits were already made. The merge would
1703		// have fast-forwarded cleanly (no sibling advance), so the test proves
1704		// execute-bead preserves based on the error signal alone, not on any
1705		// merge-side failure.
1706		directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
1707		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte(
1708			"create-file crashfile.txt pre-crash-content\n"+
1709				"commit feat: partial work before crash\n"+
1710				"fail-during 2\n",
1711		), 0o644))
1712	
1713		// Real runner + real git ops: no overrides that fake the thing under test.
1714		f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
1715		f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})
1716	
1717		root := f.NewRootCommand()
1718		out, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json",
1719			"--from", baseSHA,
1720			"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
1721		require.NoError(t, err, "execute-bead should not return an error; output: %s", out)
1722		res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
1723	
1724		assert.Equal(t, 1, res.ExitCode)
1725		assert.Equal(t, "preserved", res.Outcome)
1726		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, res.Status)
1727		assert.Equal(t, baseSHA, res.BaseRev)
1728		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.ResultRev)
1729		assert.NotEqual(t, baseSHA, res.ResultRev, "worker must have produced a commit before the failure")
1730		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.PreserveRef)
1731	
1732		// Hidden ref must resolve to the worker's commit, proving it was
1733		// preserved rather than discarded.
1734		preservedSHA := runGit("rev-parse", res.PreserveRef)
1735		assert.Equal(t, res.ResultRev, preservedSHA, "preserve ref must resolve to the worker commit")
1736	
1737		// Target branch must NOT advance — an agent error, even with commits
1738		// and a clean would-have-succeeded merge, must preserve before land.
1739		// (Pre-refactor equivalent: assert.Equal(t, 0, git.mergeCalls).)
1740		assert.Equal(t, baseSHA, runGit("rev-parse", "refs/heads/main"),
1741			"main must be unchanged when agent errors with commits")
1742		mergeCommits := runGit("log", "--merges", "--format=%H", "refs/heads/main")
1743		assert.Empty(t, mergeCommits, "agent-error path must not produce a merge commit on main")
1744	}
1745	
1746	// TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorWithCommitsPreserves verifies that when the agent
1747	// runner returns an error and commits exist, exitCode=1 and outcome="preserved"
1748	// with a non-empty, correctly-formatted preserve ref. Exercises real git +
1749	// RealLandingGitOps via the script harness — no fakes for the components under
1750	// test.
1751	func TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorWithCommitsPreserves(t *testing.T) {
1752		workDir := t.TempDir()
1753	
1754		scrubEnv := func() []string {
1755			parent := os.Environ()
1756			env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
1757			for _, kv := range parent {
1758				if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
1759					continue
1760				}
1761				env = append(env, kv)
1762			}
1763			return env
1764		}
1765		runGit := func(args ...string) string {
1766			t.Helper()
1767			cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
1768			cmd.Dir = workDir
1769			cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
1770			out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
1771			require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
1772			return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
1773		}
1774	
1775		runGit("init", "-b", "main")
1776		runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
1777		runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
1778		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
1779		runGit("add", ".")
1780		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
1781	
1782		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
1783			ID:        "my-bead",
1784			Title:     "Test execute-bead agent error with commits preserves",
1785			Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
1786			IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
1787		})
1788		runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
1789		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead")
1790		baseSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
1791	
1792		// Script harness: create a real commit in the worker worktree, then
1793		// trip a synthetic failure so runScriptFn returns (result, execErr).
1794		// Mirrors the original fakeAgentRunner{err: ..., result: nil} with
1795		// commits present.
1796		directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
1797		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte(
1798			"create-file crashfile.txt pre-crash-content\n"+
1799				"commit feat: partial work before crash\n"+
1800				"fail-during 2\n",
1801		), 0o644))
1802	
1803		// Real runner + real git ops: no overrides that fake the thing under test.
1804		f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
1805		f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})
1806	
1807		root := f.NewRootCommand()
1808		out, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json",
1809			"--from", baseSHA,
1810			"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
1811		require.NoError(t, err, "execute-bead should not return an error; output: %s", out)
1812		res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
1813	
1814		assert.Equal(t, 1, res.ExitCode)
1815		assert.Equal(t, "preserved", res.Outcome)
1816		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, res.Status)
1817		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.ResultRev)
1818		assert.NotEqual(t, baseSHA, res.ResultRev, "worker must have produced a commit before the failure")
1819		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.PreserveRef)
1820		assertPreserveRef(t, res.PreserveRef, "my-bead", baseSHA)
1821	
1822		// Preserve ref must resolve to the worker's commit.
1823		preservedSHA := runGit("rev-parse", res.PreserveRef)
1824		assert.Equal(t, res.ResultRev, preservedSHA, "preserve ref must resolve to the worker commit")
1825	}
1826	
1827	// TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorMessageInOutput verifies that when the agent runner
1828	// returns an error, the error message appears in the JSON output Error field.
1829	// Exercises real git + RealLandingGitOps via the script harness — no fakes for
1830	// the components under test.
1831	func TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorMessageInOutput(t *testing.T) {
1832		workDir := t.TempDir()
1833	
1834		scrubEnv := func() []string {
1835			parent := os.Environ()
1836			env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
1837			for _, kv := range parent {
1838				if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
1839					continue
1840				}
1841				env = append(env, kv)
1842			}
1843			return env
1844		}
1845		runGit := func(args ...string) string {
1846			t.Helper()
1847			cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
1848			cmd.Dir = workDir
1849			cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
1850			out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
1851			require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
1852			return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
1853		}

23	// fakeExecuteBeadGit is a mock implementation of executeBeadGitOps for tests.
24	type fakeExecuteBeadGit struct {
25		mu sync.Mutex
26	
27		// mainHeadRev is returned by HeadRev/ResolveRev for the main working dir.
28		mainHeadRev string
29		// headRevSeq, when set, is returned in order for successive main-dir HeadRev calls.

32		// wtHeadRev is returned by HeadRev for worktree paths (after agent run).
33		wtHeadRev string
34		// wtDirty is returned by IsDirty for worktree paths.

36		// synthRev, if set, is applied as wtHeadRev when SynthesizeCommit is called.
37		synthRev string
38		// wtHeadRevErr, if set, is returned by HeadRev for worktree paths.
39		wtHeadRevErr error
40		dirty        bool
41		mergeErr     error
42		updateRefErr error
43	
44		addedWTs   []string
45		addedWTRev string
46		removedWTs []string
47		refs       map[string]string // ref -> sha recorded by UpdateRef
48		worktrees  []string          // paths returned by WorktreeList
49	
50		mergeCalls int

229	func assertPreserveRef(t *testing.T, ref, beadID, baseRev string) {
230		t.Helper()
231		shortSHA := baseRev
232		if len(shortSHA) > 12 {
233			shortSHA = shortSHA[:12]
234		}
235		pattern := fmt.Sprintf(`^refs/ddx/iterations/%s/\d{8}T\d{6}Z-%s$`,
236			regexp.QuoteMeta(beadID), regexp.QuoteMeta(shortSHA))
237		require.Regexp(t, pattern, ref)
238	}
239	
240	// runExecuteBead invokes the execute-bead command through the cobra tree and returns
241	// the parsed JSON result. It extracts the JSON object from the combined output,
242	// skipping any leading note/warning lines written to stderr.
243	func runExecuteBead(t *testing.T, f *CommandFactory, git *fakeExecuteBeadGit, beadID string, extraArgs ...string) agent.ExecuteBeadResult {
244		t.Helper()
245		root := f.NewRootCommand()
246		args := append([]string{"agent", "execute-bead", beadID, "--json"}, extraArgs...)
247		out, err := executeCommand(root, args...)
248		require.NoError(t, err, "execute-bead should not return an error; output: %s", out)
249		return parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
250	}
251	
252	func parseExecuteBeadJSON(t *testing.T, out string) agent.ExecuteBeadResult {
253		t.Helper()
254		// Strip any non-JSON prefix lines (e.g. stderr notes written to the shared buffer).
255		jsonStart := strings.Index(out, "{")
256		require.NotEqual(t, -1, jsonStart, "output should contain JSON: %s", out)
257		jsonPart := out[jsonStart:]
258		var res agent.ExecuteBeadResult
259		dec := json.NewDecoder(bytes.NewBufferString(jsonPart))
260		require.NoError(t, dec.Decode(&res), "output should be valid JSON: %s", jsonPart)
261		return res
262	}
263	
264	func seedExecuteBead(t *testing.T, workDir string, b *bead.Bead) {
265		t.Helper()
266		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(workDir, ".ddx"))
267		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
268		if _, err := store.Get(b.ID); err == nil {
269			return
270		}
271		require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
272	}

2010		workDir := t.TempDir()
2011	
2012		scrubEnv := func() []string {
2013			parent := os.Environ()
2014			env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
2015			for _, kv := range parent {
2016				if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
2017					continue
2018				}
2019				env = append(env, kv)
2020			}
2021			return env
2022		}
2023		runGit := func(args ...string) string {
2024			t.Helper()
2025			cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
2026			cmd.Dir = workDir
2027			cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
2028			out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
2029			require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
2030			return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
2031		}
2032	
2033		runGit("init", "-b", "main")
2034		runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
2035		runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
2036		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
2037		runGit("add", ".")
2038		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
2039	
2040		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
2041			ID:        "my-bead",
2042			Title:     "Test execute-bead compound agent and HeadRev failure",
2043			Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
2044			IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
2045		})
2046		runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
2047		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead")
2048		baseSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
2049	
2050		// Script directives:
2051		//   directive 0 — delete-file .git: corrupts the worktree so post-agent
2052		//                 `git rev-parse HEAD` fails for real.
2053		//   directive 1 — fail-during 1: returns an agent-level error to the
2054		//                 worker, populating res.Error.
2055		// Together these reproduce the compound (agentErr + revErr) path without
2056		// any fakes of the components under test.
2057		directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
2058		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte(
2059			"delete-file .git\n"+
2060				"fail-during 1\n",
2061		), 0o644))
2062	
2063		// Real runner + real git ops: no overrides that fake the thing under test.
2064		f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
2065		f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})
2066	
2067		root := f.NewRootCommand()
2068		out, cmdErr := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json",
2069			"--from", baseSHA,
2070			"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
2071		require.Error(t, cmdErr)
2072		res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
2073	
2074		assert.Equal(t, 1, res.ExitCode)
2075		assert.Equal(t, "error", res.Outcome)
2076		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, res.Status)
2077		assert.Contains(t, res.Error, "synthetic failure at directive 1",
2078			"agent error message must be preserved even when HeadRev also fails")
2079		assert.Contains(t, res.Reason, "rev-parse",
2080			"Reason must reflect the real git rev-parse failure on the corrupted worktree")
2081	}

data.text: Migrated `TestExecuteBeadInvalidBeadID` to real git + real runner: each invalid-ID subtest now boots a real git repo, uses `NewCommandFactory` with `agent.NewRunner` (no fake runner, no fake git ops), drives the script harness via a directive file, asserts `invalid bead ID`, and verifies via `git worktree list` and HEAD that no execute-bead worktree was created and main did not advance. Test passes (`go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadInvalidBeadID$'`). No other tests modified.
